note: EN patch metadata (Stufe 2 translation). Arrays align by index with src/data/patches/*.json. Rendered by Stufe-3 EN patch pages.

4-0-0.tagline: Server Meshing and the lawless Pyro system.
4-0-0.summary: The biggest technical leap in years: static Server Meshing links multiple servers into a single shard for the first time, Pyro opens as a second star system — reachable via Jump Point — and the Contested Zones bring PvPvE loot areas deep into Pyro's facilities.

keyFacts.label: Server Meshing
keyFacts.value: ≈10 servers per shard · up to 500 players (previously ~100)

keyFacts.label: New star system
keyFacts.value: Pyro — 6 planets, Ruin Station, Rest Stops

keyFacts.label: System travel
keyFacts.value: Jump Point Stanton ↔ Pyro

keyFacts.label: Contested Zones
keyFacts.value: PvPvE loot zones in Pyro facilities

keyFacts.label: Wipe
keyFacts.value: Full wipe · +20,000 aUEC starting balance

features.name: Static Server Meshing
features.desc: For the first time, multiple servers together form a single shard and hand objects off to one another seamlessly — the player count per shard rises from around 100 to as many as 500.

features.name: Pyro system
features.desc: The second playable star system: lawless, ruled by gangs, with no security zones. Six planets (including Monox, Bloom, Terminus and the gas giant Pyro V), plus Ruin Station, Rest Stops and asteroid bases.

facts.label: Planets
facts.value: 6 — incl. Monox, Bloom, Terminus, Pyro V

facts.label: Stations
facts.value: Ruin Station · Rest Stops · asteroid bases

features.name: Contested Zones
features.desc: Sealed-off PvPvE areas deep inside Pyro's facilities: AI factions, rival players and high-tier loot — only those who make it out alive keep the spoils.

features.name: Jump Point travel
features.desc: Inter-system travel between Stanton and Pyro through the first active Jump Point.

events.name: Save Stanton — Chapter 1
events.desc: The opening of the faction narrative centered on saving Stanton; no dedicated live event.

4-0-1.tagline: Crash fixes, entity streaming and Save Stanton Chapter 2 with a permanent faction choice.
4-0-1.summary: A point release with targeted top-crash/deadlock fixes following the 4.0.0 launch. The Entity Density Manager optimizes memory management in densely populated zones, and streaming fixes address problems with elevators and hangars. Save Stanton Chapter 2 (Fight for Pyro) introduces a permanent faction choice — Citizens for Prosperity, Headhunters or Frontier Fighters — with a Ripper SMG reward for finishing in time.

keyFacts.label: Crash fixes
keyFacts.value: Top 10 most common client & server crashes fixed

keyFacts.label: Entity Density Manager
keyFacts.value: Optimized cleanup logic for densely populated zones

keyFacts.label: Streaming improvements
keyFacts.value: More reliable loading of elevators, hangars & interiors

keyFacts.label: Save Stanton Chapter 2
keyFacts.value: Fight for Pyro: permanent faction choice (3 factions)

keyFacts.label: Ripper SMG
keyFacts.value: Exclusive reward before the 4.0.2 launch

features.name: Entity Density Manager
features.desc: Optimization of the entity cleanup logic: abandoned objects and accumulated game objects are cleaned up more effectively, improving performance and server stability in densely populated zones.

features.name: Streaming fixes
features.desc: Improvements to Persistent Entity Streaming: elevators, hangars and interiors load in more reliably, with less missing geometry and fewer stalls when entering.

features.name: Top crash/deadlock fixes
features.desc: 4.0.1 was a stability point release with targeted fixes against the most common client and server crashes of the 4.0 launch, particularly deadlock scenarios.

features.name: Save Stanton Chapter 2: Fight for Pyro
features.desc: A dynamic event with a permanent faction choice: after the first mission, players commit to Citizens for Prosperity, Headhunters or Frontier Fighters. From then on all missions are reputation-bound. Completing it before 4.0.2 rewards an exclusive Ripper SMG.

events.name: Fight for Pyro (Save Stanton Chapter 2)
events.desc: A dynamic event with a permanent faction choice in Pyro. Players choose one of three factions (Citizens for Prosperity, Headhunters, Frontier Fighters) during the first mission and fight reputation-bound to the end of the campaign. A Ripper SMG reward is granted for completion before the 4.0.2 launch.

4-0-2.tagline: A supply war over Pyro: Citizens for Prosperity vs. Headhunters.
4-0-2.summary: Supply or Die was a one-time PvPvE supply event in Pyro in which players joined one of two factions: Citizens for Prosperity (stabilization through industrial goods and Detatrine) or Headhunters (chaos and salvage piracy). Through depot battles, mining and salvage, players fed material into the war effort and thereby decided who ultimately controlled the lawless system.

keyFacts.label: Warring factions
keyFacts.value: Citizens for Prosperity vs. Headhunters

keyFacts.label: Supply war
keyFacts.value: PvPvE depot battles for control of Pyro

keyFacts.label: Key resource
keyFacts.value: Detatrine — the Citizens' rare chemical

keyFacts.label: Mission types
keyFacts.value: 3 paths: depot battles, mining & refining, salvage

keyFacts.label: Tier 2 main reward
keyFacts.value: Ravager-212 Outcast twin shotgun

features.name: Supply or Die event
features.desc: A one-time, story-driven PvPvE supply event in Pyro's depots. Players pick a faction and gather material through combat, mining and salvage — every delivery shifts the war effort.

features.name: Faction resupply economy
features.desc: A dynamic delivery system with two competing factions: Citizens for Prosperity gather industrial goods and Detatrine for medical supplies; Headhunters procure salvage and mining raw materials by any means necessary.

features.name: PvPvE depot battles
features.desc: Players fight at contested Pyro depots against AI and rival players for supplies for their faction.

facts.label: Depots
facts.value: Contested Pyro facilities with PvPvE firefights

features.name: Ravager-212 Outcast shotgun
features.desc: A twin-shotgun reward (event Tier 2). Paired with Outcast skins for the MISC Fortune, Drake Vulture and MISC Prospector (Tier 3).

facts.label: Tier 3 cosmetics
facts.value: Outcast paints for Fortune, Vulture, Prospector

events.name: Supply or Die
events.desc: A one-time PvPvE supply event: players choose between Citizens for Prosperity (stabilizing Pyro) and Headhunters (chaos). Through depot battles, mining and salvage they delivered material that shifted the war effort and decided who controlled the lawless system.

4-1-0.tagline: Orbital Hathor lasers, asteroid mining and new collision physics.
4-1-0.summary: The persistent "Align & Mine" activity on Aberdeen and Daymar: players align ground-based Hathor Alignment Facilities, whereupon the Orbital Mining Station fires a laser and blasts open brand-new mineral-rich caves. Alongside this, 4.1.0 brings reworked collision physics (mass/velocity), the Aegis Idris as a playable frigate and the new Valakkar fauna.

keyFacts.label: Persistent activity
keyFacts.value: Align & Mine · Aberdeen & Daymar

keyFacts.label: Hathor facilities
keyFacts.value: 2 moons · 1 Orbital Mining Station each

keyFacts.label: Playable ships
keyFacts.value: Aegis Idris (frigate) for owners

keyFacts.label: Bug fixes
keyFacts.value: ~130 documented corrections

keyFacts.label: New fauna
keyFacts.value: Juvenile Valakkar (burrowing worm creatures)

features.name: Align & Mine
features.desc: A persistent cooperative sandbox activity: players align ground-based Hathor Alignment Facilities on Aberdeen and Daymar, whereupon the Orbital Mining Station fires a laser and blasts open a brand-new, mineral-rich cave.

features.name: Reworked collision physics
features.desc: An expanded collision system that gives greater weight to the mass and velocity of the objects involved, for more realistic crash and impact behavior.

features.name: Aegis Idris (playable)
features.desc: The Aegis Idris-M and Idris-P become fully playable for their owners for the first time in the Persistent Universe.

features.name: Juvenile Valakkar fauna
features.desc: New burrowing worm creatures on some moons; they keep their distance from players, move through the ground and pelt players with rock.

features.name: NPC interactions & facial DNA
features.desc: New NPC interaction options and expanded DNA-based facial generators for diverse NPC characters.

features.name: Arena Commander maps
features.desc: New maps added for Arena Commander.

features.name: Server Meshing mission adjustments
features.desc: Many missions were reworked and adjusted for compatibility with static Server Meshing.

features.name: Wikelo's Emporium
features.desc: Alpha 4.1 introduces Wikelo — a Banu trader and the first playable alien NPC in Star Citizen. His Emporium runs as a pure barter system with no money: players accept contracts, deliver the requested items and materials at the freight elevator and receive rewards from Wikelo's stock in return. The currency is "Wikelo Favor" tokens, spread across three asteroid stations in Stanton.

4-1-1.tagline: Weapon balancing, recoil and Fleet Week with Hunt The Polaris.
4-1-1.summary: A point release focused on weapon systems: the new recoil system brings visible recoil reactions when firing, turret ammunition capacity was significantly increased and over 200 bug fixes stabilized the game. Hunt The Polaris, a server-wide mission during Fleet Week 2955, has the entire shard collectively chase down a stolen RSI Polaris and share the spoils.

keyFacts.label: Bug fixes
keyFacts.value: >200 corrections (61 from the Issue Council)

keyFacts.label: Turret ammunition
keyFacts.value: Significantly increased (Redeemer, Retaliator, Reclaimer, Carrack, Polaris, Hammerhead)

keyFacts.label: Recoil system
keyFacts.value: Newly implemented, actor-based, scalable/toggleable

keyFacts.label: Fleet Week 2955
keyFacts.value: Invictus expo · server-wide mission Hunt The Polaris

keyFacts.label: Weapon balancing
keyFacts.value: Extensive tuning of ship and personal weapons

features.name: Recoil system (new)
features.desc: A newly implemented recoil system with actor-based effects when firing ship and vehicle weapons. The effects are visible, scalable and can be turned off — every shot feels more controlled and tangible.

features.name: Turret ammunition increased
features.desc: The ammunition capacity of turrets was significantly increased. Ships especially affected: Redeemer, Retaliator, Reclaimer, Carrack, Polaris and Hammerhead. Some individual turret weapons were also retuned for higher damage.

facts.label: Affected ships
facts.value: Redeemer, Retaliator, Reclaimer, Carrack, Polaris, Hammerhead

features.name: Weapon balancing
features.desc: Extensive tuning of ship and personal weapons: damage values, ranges and firing behavior were rebalanced to make combat feel fairer and more consistent.

features.name: Hunt The Polaris (event)
features.desc: A server-wide mission during Fleet Week 2955: the Frontier Fighters steal an RSI Polaris in the middle of the fleet show. The entire server chases the stolen corvette across Stanton to a fleeting wormhole, where the decisive battle takes place. The spoils are shared among all participants.

facts.label: Theft
facts.value: RSI Polaris by the Frontier Fighters

features.name: Fleet Week 2955 (Invictus)
features.desc: The annual fleet parade and Invictus expo, where the UEE's largest ships stand side by side. Hunt The Polaris is embedded in this setting — while the fleet show is underway, the Frontier Fighters strike.

events.name: Hunt The Polaris
events.desc: A server-wide mission: the Frontier Fighters steal an RSI Polaris during Fleet Week 2955. The whole server chases the stolen corvette across Stanton to the wormhole battle. The reward is shared.

events.name: Fleet Week 2955 (Invictus)
events.desc: The UEE's annual fleet show and expo featuring the largest ships. Hunt The Polaris plays out against this backdrop.

4-1-1.fixesNote: >200 bug fixes (61 from the Issue Council); a balancing and stability point release

4-2-0.tagline: Persistent storm activity, dynamic rain, and the new environmental hazard: radiation.
4-2-0.summary: The sky becomes the enemy: Alpha 4.2.0 adds dynamic weather to Stanton and Pyro — heavy rain lowers visibility, turbulence rattles flight stability, and a brand-new environmental hazard, radiation, builds up insidiously over time. The namesake Storm Breaker mission sends players into the heart of these storms, where only the CureLife DeconPen Xtra protects against the deadly radiation exposure.

keyFacts.label: Dynamic weather
keyFacts.value: Heavy rain + turbulence at Stanton & Pyro

keyFacts.label: Radiation
keyFacts.value: New environmental hazard — builds up over time, deadly without protection

keyFacts.label: Antidote
keyFacts.value: CureLife DeconPen Xtra (Canoiodide + Hemozal)

keyFacts.label: VTOL mechanics
keyFacts.value: Hovering and vertical landing made significantly harder in a storm

features.name: Dynamic Rain & Weather
features.desc: With 4.2.0, weather at Stanton and Pyro locations becomes an active gameplay mechanic: dynamic rain drastically lowers visibility, storm fronts bring strong wind and turbulence that reduce flight stability and destabilize precision approaches.

features.name: Radiation — new environmental hazard
features.desc: A brand-new and insidious environmental hazard: radiation builds up over time in affected zones. It is invisible and deadly once exposure reaches its maximum. Retreating from the zone lets the radiation dissipate again — but anyone who waits too long has already lost.

features.name: Storm Breaker — persistent activity
features.desc: The namesake mission leads players deep into the storms — a persistent activity tied to the ongoing rain-crisis story. Anyone who flies into the storm is seeking a fight with the elements.

features.name: In-flight turbulence
features.desc: Storms generate flight turbulence that lowers flight stability. Every gust tugs at your course, and VTOL maneuvers (hovering and vertical landing) are especially tricky, becoming a nerve-wracking ordeal in a storm.

features.name: CureLife DeconPen Xtra
features.desc: A quick injector serving as an antidote to radiation. It administers Canoiodide and Hemozal directly into the bloodstream, buying the pilot time against rising radiation exposure. Mandatory gear in the storm loadout as of 4.2.0.

events.name: Storm Breaker
events.desc: Persistent activity deep in the storm; tied to the rain-crisis story. Not a dedicated, time-limited live event.

topics.title: Storm Breaker · Weather becomes a hazard

4-2-1.tagline: A global convoy event with four competing megacorporations, ship-escort missions, and the RSI Meteor.
4-2-1.summary: The first true global event with cross-shard tracking: Imperator Addison calls on Stanton's four megacorporations to compete for research material. Players pick a side and contribute through hauling, reclaim missions, and convoy escort. Hurston Dynamics wins with roughly 337 million SCU. New ship-escort missions (6 tiers, Foxwell group) and the RSI Meteor debut as a logistics freighter.

keyFacts.label: Global event
keyFacts.value: First event with cross-shard tracking

keyFacts.label: Competing corporations
keyFacts.value: 4 (Hurston Dynamics, Crusader Industries, ArcCorp, microTech)

keyFacts.label: Winner's delivery total
keyFacts.value: Hurston Dynamics: ~337 million SCU

keyFacts.label: Reward tiers
keyFacts.value: 5 tiers · T5: Strata Hurston Black + 5% permanent discount

keyFacts.label: Ship-escort missions
keyFacts.value: 6 difficulty tiers · Foxwell group · Scrip

features.name: Resource Drive
features.desc: The first global event with cross-shard tracking: four megacorporations compete over how much research material they deliver. Players pick a side and contribute through hauling, reclaim missions, and convoy escort. Progress is combined across all servers.

features.name: Ship-escort missions
features.desc: A new mission type: players defend the transport ships of friendly vessels against enemy attackers, escorting them to and from asteroid mining bases until the quantum-travel getaway. Six difficulty tiers from the Foxwell group in Stanton and Pyro, with Scrip rewards.

features.name: Convoy routes
features.desc: Escorted cargo trains around the mining bases in Stanton and Pyro. Without protection from a player escort, the cargo won't reach the collection point. Central to Resource Drive progress.

features.name: Wikelo recipes
features.desc: Reworked crafting system: recipes are organized into three ingredient tiers — guaranteed core drops, very rare items, and content-bound items. Random rewards are gone, giving clear visibility into what's worth collecting.

facts.label: What's new
facts.value: Deterministic structure instead of random drops

features.name: Flight Tuning
features.desc: Fine-tuning of flight behavior across all ships; optimizes handling and responsive feedback beyond the event update itself.

events.name: Resource Drive
events.desc: A global competitive event between four megacorporations over how much research material they deliver. Players pick a corporation and contribute to the total through hauling and convoy escort. Winner: Hurston Dynamics with roughly 337 million SCU. The first event with cross-shard tracking.

4-3-0.tagline: Personal Hangars and ~100 abandoned Onyx Facilities.
4-3-0.summary: Alpha 4.3.0 introduces Personal Hangars — instanced home ports where players spawn directly and get immediate access to their ships. In parallel, around 100 abandoned ASD research stations (Onyx Facilities) appear scattered across Stanton. The mission giver Arken Mallor kicks off investigation chains that uncover Dr. Logan Jorrit's experiments in regeneration and drive the Regen Crisis narrative forward. Flight tuning for light fighters and the ongoing Resource Drive event round out the expansion.

keyFacts.label: Onyx Facilities
keyFacts.value: ~100 abandoned ASD research stations · Stanton-wide

keyFacts.label: Personal Hangars
keyFacts.value: Instanced home ports · direct spawning

keyFacts.label: Mission giver
keyFacts.value: Arken Mallor (Hockrow Agency)

keyFacts.label: New ship
keyFacts.value: Kruger L-21 Wolf · light fighter

keyFacts.label: Narrative
keyFacts.value: Regen Crisis · Dr. Logan Jorrit's experiments

features.name: Personal Hangars
features.desc: Players get personal, instanced hangars as a fixed home location. They spawn directly in their own hangar instead of via NPC habs, with immediate access to ships and inventory at the set home hangar.

features.name: Onyx Facilities
features.desc: Around 100 abandoned ASD research stations scattered across Stanton. Unsecured interiors full of data traces, audio logs, and loot — tied to the Regen Crisis story under the direction of Dr. Logan Jorrit.

features.name: Investigation missions
features.desc: New investigation and data-salvage missions via mobiGlas from Arken Mallor (Hockrow Agency). Players enter Onyx Facilities, solve puzzles, and recover data to uncover the truth behind the Regen Crisis.

features.name: Flight Tuning Light-Fighter
features.desc: Reworked flight mechanics specifically for light fighters like the new Kruger L-21 Wolf.

features.name: Resource Drive
features.desc: An ongoing event for hauling resources, especially Aphorite.

events.name: Resource Drive
events.desc: An ongoing event with hauling missions and rewards.

4-3-1.tagline: RSI Apollo, medical overhaul, and the MedGel system.
4-3-1.summary: 4.3.1 reworks the medical system from the ground up: the RSI Apollo medical ship with modular medical configurations becomes the centerpiece, MedGel becomes a consumed resource for respawns and healing, and bed tiers T1–T3 define new respawn distances. The Onyx story expands with Site B and Dr. Jorrit's lab, where biological samples reveal the next layer of the mystery.

keyFacts.label: RSI Apollo
keyFacts.value: 2 modular medical rooms, Medivac & Triage configurations

keyFacts.label: MedGel costs
keyFacts.value: T3=5 / T2=10 / T1=20 charges per respawn/healing

keyFacts.label: Bed tiers
keyFacts.value: T1–T3 with adjusted respawn distances

keyFacts.label: Onyx expansion
keyFacts.value: Site B – Dr. Jorrit's lab with biological samples

features.name: Medical Overhaul
features.desc: A comprehensive rework of the medical system: wounds are created under new rules, healing and rescue are re-mechanized, and revival works via bed tiers with defined respawn distances.

features.name: MedGel system
features.desc: A new consumable for medical beds: respawning and healing at med beds now consume tiered charges of docked MedGels — from triage to full revival. Beds go idle when MedGel runs out.

features.name: RSI Apollo medical ship
features.desc: A dedicated medical ship with two modular configurations: Medivac for recovering the wounded from the field, Triage for onboard care with regeneration capabilities. A flying base of operations for teams deep in the Verse.

facts.label: Highlight
facts.value: 2 modular medical rooms with regeneration capabilities

features.name: Onyx Site B
features.desc: An expansion of the Onyx Facilities: Site B opens Dr. Jorrit's lab, where players must recover biological samples to understand the full context of the experiments. Another layer of the investigative Onyx story.

features.name: Atmospheric Control Surfaces
features.desc: A flight-model improvement for more realistic atmospheric flight behavior, especially on the new medical ships.

features.name: Ship Respawn via Dropships & Watchtowers
features.desc: An alternative respawn mechanic for destroyed ships via dropship or watchtower deployments instead of the classic claim wait time.

ships.notes: Two modular medical rooms, regeneration capabilities

4-3-2.tagline: Frontier Fighters finale, Salvage rework and the Yormandi boss.
4-3-2.summary: The final episode of the Pyro Must Burn story: across three phases, the Headhunters and Citizens for Prosperity hunt the Frontier Fighters led by Amelia Boyd across Stanton and Pyro — culminating in a server-wide coordinated Polaris takedown. The patch also brings a comprehensive Salvage and Refining rework, the Yormandi boss in the Onyx caves (ASD Site B) and the Anvil Paladin as a new heavy gunship.

keyFacts.label: Phases
keyFacts.value: 3 (Reconnaissance → Siege → Takedown)

keyFacts.label: Frontier Fighters finale
keyFacts.value: Server-wide mission; Polaris takedown in phase 3

keyFacts.label: Salvage rework
keyFacts.value: Material value increased; refining system expanded

keyFacts.label: Yormandi boss
keyFacts.value: Snake-like creature in Onyx Site B; solo/duo fight

keyFacts.label: Anvil Paladin
keyFacts.value: New heavy gunship with multiple turrets

keyFacts.label: Faction alliance
keyFacts.value: Headhunters + Citizens for Prosperity against Amelia Boyd's Frontier Fighters

features.name: Frontier Fighters Finale
features.desc: Three-phase finale of the Pyro Must Burn story: phase 1 reconnaissance of Frontier Fighters movements, phase 2 siege of their strongholds, phase 3 a server-wide coordinated Polaris takedown. The Headhunters and Citizens for Prosperity factions work together against the Frontier Fighters led by Amelia Boyd.

features.name: Salvage rework & Refining
features.desc: Structural rework of the Salvage system with increased material values and an expanded refining economy. Salvage becomes economically more attractive; players benefit from an improved loot-to-credit ratio.

features.name: Yormandi boss fight
features.desc: A new snake-like, biomechanical creature in the Onyx facilities (ASD Site B). A multi-phase boss fight that is doable solo but noticeably easier in a duo. Wikelo contracts demand the creature's eyes and tongues as rewards.

features.name: Anvil Paladin
features.desc: A new heavy gunship from Anvil with multiple turret positions and a crew deck. Designed for coordinated multi-crew combat missions and hunting operations.

features.name: Combat & FPS updates
features.desc: Reworks of the melee and firearms systems as well as FPS mechanics to support the Frontier Fighters missions.

features.name: Economy & Trading tuning
features.desc: Adjustments to trade routes, prices and mission rewards for the economy of Stanton and Pyro.

ships.notes: Heavy gunship with multiple turrets and a crew deck

events.name: Frontier Fighters Finale
events.desc: Three-phase finale of the Pyro Must Burn story; server-wide hunt for a stolen Polaris in phase 3; Headhunters and Citizens for Prosperity as an allied alliance led by Amelia Boyd.

4-3-2.fixesNote: Numerous stability and gameplay fixes; exact number not specified

4-4-0.tagline: Third system Nyx, first Vanduul AI and the IAE 2955.
4-4-0.summary: Nyx opens as the third fully simulated star system — fog-shrouded, lawless, asteroid-dense. The Vanduul appear for the first time as AI enemies in the persistent universe, patrolling with Stinger, Glaive, Scythe and Blade. Levski flourishes as a rock city on Delamar. IAE 2955 in Orison puts 200+ ships up for testing — free until December 3. Interstellar hauling connects cross-system missions for the first time.

keyFacts.label: Third star system
keyFacts.value: Nyx — Glaciem Ring, Levski on Delamar

keyFacts.label: Vanduul in the PU
keyFacts.value: 4 AI ship types: Stinger, Glaive, Scythe, Blade

keyFacts.label: IAE 2955
keyFacts.value: 200+ ships free to test · Free-Fly until Dec 3, 2025

keyFacts.label: Interstellar hauling
keyFacts.value: Missions spanning Stanton, Pyro and Nyx

keyFacts.label: Operation Sworn Enemies
keyFacts.value: Investigation mission into Vanduul technology

keyFacts.label: Fixes
keyFacts.value: 180+ corrections

features.name: Nyx system
features.desc: The third fully simulated star system: dark, fog-shrouded, asteroid-dense. Tied to Stanton and Pyro via Jump Points. Home to the Glaciem Ring and the rock city of Levski.

features.name: Glaciem Ring
features.desc: A dense, dark, fog-shrouded asteroid belt running through the Nyx system. Hunting grounds of the Vanduul, tight visibility, hard to traverse.

features.name: Levski
features.desc: A landing zone on Delamar, an asteroid of the Nyx system. A city in the rock, seat of the Peoples Alliance. The only fixed foothold in lawless Nyx.

features.name: Vanduul as AI enemies
features.desc: For the first time the Vanduul enter the persistent universe as a standalone AI enemy faction. Four ship types — Stinger (light), Glaive, Scythe, Blade — patrol Nyx and attack without warning.

features.name: Operation Sworn Enemies
features.desc: An investigation mission in the abandoned stations of the Nyx system. Players gather clues about Vanduul technology and run straight into the aliens in the process — the contract can turn into a firefight at any moment.

features.name: Interstellar hauling
features.desc: Procedural cargo missions that start in one system and end in another. Connects Stanton, Pyro and Nyx with cross-system trade chains.

features.name: IAE 2955
features.desc: Intergalactic Aerospace Expo in Orison. 200+ ships from all manufacturers free to try. Free-Fly until December 3, 2025. The largest ship show in the Verse.

events.name: IAE 2955
events.desc: Intergalactic Aerospace Expo in Orison with 200+ ships free to try. Free-Fly until December 3, 2025.

events.name: Operation Sworn Enemies
events.desc: An investigation mission to explore Vanduul technology in the abandoned stations of the Nyx system. Players run straight into the new AI enemies for the first time in the process.

4-5-0.tagline: Engineering gameplay, Ship Armor and spreading fire.
4-5-0.summary: Engineering becomes gameplay with 4.5.0: players manage power in real time, Ship Armor absorbs damage as its own layer and fires spread dynamically through ships. Vulkan becomes the default render pipeline (replacing DirectX 11) with experimental VR support. A crew-centered rework of ship combat mechanics.

keyFacts.label: New engineering systems
keyFacts.value: 4 — Engineering, Ship Armor, Fire Hazard, Power Management

keyFacts.label: Ship Armor
keyFacts.value: Its own physical layer; absorbs damage before the hull; wears down over time

keyFacts.label: Fire Hazard
keyFacts.value: Spreads dynamically, consumes oxygen, requires active firefighting

keyFacts.label: Default renderer
keyFacts.value: Vulkan (replaces DirectX 11; available experimentally since 3.23)

keyFacts.label: VR support
keyFacts.value: Experimental · via the Vulkan pipeline

keyFacts.label: Power Management
keyFacts.value: Real-time power distribution; overload leads to heat and failures

features.name: Engineering gameplay
features.desc: Ship operation becomes active gameplay: players monitor their ship's condition at the engineering terminal in real time and repair damaged components while in operation. In multi-crew ships a new crew role emerges.

facts.label: Terminal control
facts.value: Real-time monitoring and repair on board

facts.label: Crew experience
facts.value: Ships become a cooperative tool with defined roles

features.name: Ship Armor
features.desc: Ship armor becomes its own physical layer above the hull and components. The armor absorbs incoming damage before it reaches the structure, wears down over time and makes damage management a strategic decision.

features.name: Fire Hazard
features.desc: With 4.5.0 fires spread dynamically through the ship, consume oxygen and jump to adjacent rooms. Crews must actively fight fires instead of passively ignoring them.

features.name: Power Management
features.desc: Players distribute power in real time across components and systems. Over- or misallocation leads to heat, failures and damage — power becomes a scarce resource in ship operation.

facts.label: Priorities
facts.value: Shields, engines, weapons compete for power

features.name: Vulkan renderer
features.desc: With 4.5.0 Vulkan becomes the default render pipeline and replaces DirectX 11. The Vulkan API had been available experimentally since Alpha 3.23; 4.5.0 stabilized it (multithreaded rendering, HDR support) and made it the standard.

features.name: VR support
features.desc: With 4.5.0 experimental virtual reality support was introduced, enabled by the Vulkan render pipeline. An early, optional step toward an immersive cockpit experience.

4-6-0.tagline: Plague event at Levski, LAMP night vision and the RSI Hermes.
4-6-0.summary: The asteroid colony Levski falls under quarantine due to the Molina Mold (People's Ailment), triggered by faulty air filters. Using the new LAMP night vision (Light Amplification), players fly supply missions against pirates of the Moraine and deliver cures. The RSI Hermes (blockade-runner freighter) and the Argo Moth (salvage ship) become newly available. Over 160 bugfixes further stabilize server meshing.

keyFacts.label: Event
keyFacts.value: Lifeline for Levski — biohazard quarantine until March 17, 2026

keyFacts.label: New ships
keyFacts.value: RSI Hermes (~288 SCU freighter) · Argo Moth (Salvage)

keyFacts.label: LAMP vision
keyFacts.value: Cockpit night vision for environments around infected Levski

keyFacts.label: Molina Mold
keyFacts.value: Aggressive mold caused by faulty air filters

keyFacts.label: Bugfixes
keyFacts.value: 160+ stability and bug fixes

keyFacts.label: Supply missions
keyFacts.value: Clearing the Air: med deliveries against The Moraine (pirates)

features.name: Lifeline for Levski Event
features.desc: Pandemic event at the asteroid colony Levski. The Molina Mold was released through defective air filters and causes People's Ailment. Players must supply the quarantined colony while pirates operating out of The Moraine attack the convoys. The event runs for a limited time until March 17, 2026.

facts.label: Enemy
facts.value: The Moraine — pirates from asteroid settlements in the Glaciem Ring

features.name: LAMP (Light Amplification)
features.desc: A new cockpit night vision feature. LAMP amplifies existing residual light and enables navigation and flight in dark environments — especially useful when approaching Levski, infected by the Molina Mold, in the dark Nyx system.

facts.label: Application
facts.value: Flight and navigation in dark environments

features.name: Clearing the Air — Supply missions
features.desc: New missions for the Lifeline event. Players transport medical supplies, antifungals and filter/cure manufacturing materials to quarantined Levski while The Moraine pirates attack the convoys. Convoy protection required.

features.name: RSI Hermes
features.desc: A new RSI blockade-runner freighter. The Hermes is specialized for fast supply runs under dangerous conditions and becomes the main ship of the supply chains during the Lifeline event.

facts.label: Function
facts.value: Fast supply runs in crisis situations

features.name: Argo Moth
features.desc: A medium salvage ship from Argo Astronautics. The Moth is specialized in salvage and is set to become available in resupply after release (from February 11, 2026).

features.name: Engineering & Armor refinement
features.desc: Continuation of the engineering mechanics introduced in 4.5.0. Optimizations to power distribution and damage calculation for ship armor; stabilization of the systems after the patch launch.

features.name: Place-from-Inventory reactivated
features.desc: The feature for directly placing items from the inventory is reactivated after having been disabled in previous patches.

features.name: Star Map harvestable data
features.desc: The Star Map gains data on harvestable resource spots (harvestables) to give players better planning of mining and gathering missions.

facts.label: Data
facts.value: Harvestable locations available in Star Map

features.name: Kel-To ship supply kiosks
features.desc: New supply kiosks enable on-site ship resupply, relevant for the fast supply runs of the Lifeline event.

features.name: 160+ stability and bugfixes
features.desc: An extensive bugfix package for server meshing stability, streaming, NPC interactions and general game stability — typical for major patches.

events.name: Lifeline for Levski
events.desc: Pandemic event: the asteroid colony Levski is stricken by the Molina Mold (People's Ailment). Players carry out supply missions (Clearing the Air) while The Moraine pirates attack the convoys. The event runs for a limited time until March 17, 2026.

4-6-0.fixesNote: 160+ bugfixes and stability improvements for server meshing and general game systems.

4-7-0.tagline: Crafting T0, Operation Breaker Stations in the Keeger Belt, and the modular Aurora Mk II.
4-7-0.summary: The Keeger Belt opens up as an asteroid belt in the Nyx system. With Crafting T0, players can for the first time build FPS weapons and armor themselves from blueprints and rare minerals. Operation Breaker Stations is a multi-stage co-op mission to reactivate Shubin Breaker stations. The Aurora Mk II replaces the old Aurora model with modular combat or cargo configurations. The seasonal Stella Fortuna event brings racing and cosmetics.

keyFacts.label: Crafting Tier
keyFacts.value: T0 · FPS weapons & armor from blueprints

keyFacts.label: Operation Breaker Stations
keyFacts.value: Multi-stage co-op in the Keeger Belt · PvE/PvP variants

keyFacts.label: Aurora Mk II
keyFacts.value: Modular starter · +6 SCU cargo or combat module

keyFacts.label: Keeger Belt
keyFacts.value: Asteroid belt in Nyx with QV Breaker stations

keyFacts.label: Stella Fortuna
keyFacts.value: Seasonal event · racing, video contest, cosmetics

features.name: Crafting T0
features.desc: First crafting tier: build FPS weapons and armor from blueprints and rare minerals. Material quality influences the item's final stats.

features.name: Operation Breaker Stations
features.desc: Multi-stage co-op mission in the Keeger Belt: players reactivate decommissioned Shubin Breaker stations, fire a laser to break apart asteroids, and craft optical components. Playable as PvE mode (exclusive) or open FFA PvP.

features.name: Aurora Mk II
features.desc: New modular starter ship from RSI with swappable modules: standard configuration, +6 SCU cargo module, or combat module with shield & missiles. Replaces the old Aurora model.

features.name: Inventory Rework
features.desc: Major overhaul cycle of the inventory system to support Crafting and Operation Breaker Stations.

features.name: Combat Overhaul
features.desc: Reworked combat system focused on shield, armor, and radar balance for Operation Breaker mission requirements.

events.name: Stella Fortuna
events.desc: Seasonal luck and celebration event with Arena Commander racing, a video contest, and rewards (racing gear, cosmetics).

4-8-0.tagline: G-force tolerance, the Drake Ironclad, and the return of XenoThreat.
4-8-0.summary: Alpha 4.8.0 brings the G-force flight model with greyout/redout/blackout, Drake's first heavy armored haulers (Ironclad & Assault), coordinated Tactical Strike Group missions for organized orgs, the reworked refueling system, and the return of the massive XenoThreat event — accompanied by more than 166 bug fixes and the first full wipe since 4.0.0.

keyFacts.label: G-Force Flight Model
keyFacts.value: Grey-out → Red-out → Black-out; suit & armor decisive for tolerance

keyFacts.label: Drake Ironclad & Assault
keyFacts.value: Drake's first heavy armored haulers; Assault variant for combat

keyFacts.label: Tactical Strike Groups
keyFacts.value: Coordinated multi-ship missions in Nyx; 7+ players; Vanduul Mauler boss fight

keyFacts.label: XenoThreat Assault
keyFacts.value: Return of the wave defense event; defend the UEES War Hammer

keyFacts.label: Wipe
keyFacts.value: Full wipe (first since 4.0.0)

keyFacts.label: Bugfixes
keyFacts.value: 166+ fixes & stability improvements

features.name: G-Force Flight Model
features.desc: High-intensity G-forces directly affect pilots: positive Gs lead through greyout to blackout, negative Gs to redout. Specialized flight and racing suits with different G-tolerance values determine the strain limit and become a tactical factor.

facts.label: Greyout/Redout/Blackout
facts.value: Progressive vision impairment depending on G-load & suit type

facts.label: Suit-Dependent
facts.value: Flight suits, racing suits, and armor affect G-tolerance

features.name: Drake Ironclad & Ironclad Assault
features.desc: Drake's first true armored ship and heavy hauler: massively armored, with substantial cargo capacity and a combat configuration. The Assault variant is optimized for coordinated Strike Group battles. Plus a Drake Command Module for tactical coordination.

features.name: Tactical Strike Groups
features.desc: New cooperative multi-ship missions for organized orgs: a multi-phase raid with ship combat, tunnel flight, core destruction, and FPS infiltration of the QV Extraction Stations in Nyx. A Vanduul Mauler boss in the finale. Rewards: an exclusive crossbow blueprint and high-value loot.

features.name: XenoThreat — Assault on Stanton
features.desc: Return of the massive server-wide coordination event: a human-supremacist outlaw faction of deserted UEE Navy soldiers raids a corporate convoy and then attacks the UEES War Hammer at INS Jericho. Players recover Starfarer supplies and defend the flagship front against waves of fighters, Hammerhead gunships, and Idris-M frigates.

facts.label: Scenario
facts.value: Corporate convoy raid → supply recovery → flagship defense

features.name: Refueling Rework
features.desc: Reworked fuel mechanics with an improved UI and more realistic resource consumption models; integrated into the ship service system (T0).

features.name: Vehicle Loadout Recovery
features.desc: Players can load and save stored ship and vehicle configurations (weapons, modules, equipment); integrated with Long-Term Persistence.

features.name: Ship Hangar Service (T0)
features.desc: First iteration of hangar-based maintenance and repair services.

features.name: New Equipment & Gear
features.desc: Introduction of specialized equipment for tactical operations: the UltiFlex crossbow, Kastak plasma grenade, and Tailwind flight suit; all optimized for Strike Group scenarios.

features.name: Stability & Bug Fixes
features.desc: Over 166 bug fixes and stability improvements accompanying the new systems; full wipe for reinitialization.

features.name: Mining
features.desc: Resource extraction from hand-mined gems to multicrew ship mining. The system spans the Prospector (since Alpha 3.2), the reworked ARGO MOLE (4.7/4.8), ROC and hand mining; Pyro widened the ore pool (Stileron, Riccite, Lindinium). Raw ore is refined at refinery decks using one of nine methods and sold.

ships.notes: Established in 4.8.0 as a Gold Standard rework

events.name: Tactical Strike Groups
events.desc: New cooperative mission series for organized orgs in Nyx; a multi-phase PvE raid with ship combat, FPS, and a boss encounter.

events.name: XenoThreat — Assault on Stanton
events.desc: Return of the server-wide event with a convoy raid, supply recovery, and flagship defense against an escalating wave bombardment.

4-8-1.tagline: New mission type Defend Location — space combat over 385 asteroid bases with bomber defense and blueprints.
4-8-1.summary: Defend Location introduces a new wave-based space combat mission type: players defend asteroid mining bases against bomber runs across 385 distributed deployment sites in Stanton and Pyro. The hybrid variant combines defense with escort elements. With 4.8.1, unique crafting blueprints beckon as rewards for the first time. The point release brings 30+ stability improvements after the bumpy 4.8.0 launch.

keyFacts.label: Defend Location Deployment Sites
keyFacts.value: 385 across Stanton & Pyro

keyFacts.label: Defendable Bases
keyFacts.value: 149 locations · 6 difficulty tiers

keyFacts.label: Contractors
keyFacts.value: Foxwell, Head Hunters, CFP, Rough and Ready

keyFacts.label: Rewards
keyFacts.value: aUEC, reputation, org scrip, crafting blueprints

keyFacts.label: Stability Improvements
keyFacts.value: 30+ fixes after 4.8.0

features.name: Defend Location
features.desc: New wave-based space combat mission type: players defend asteroid mining bases against enemy bomber runs. Every bomber that gets through causes damage to the infrastructure. The hybrid variant combines defense with escort elements — friendly ships must be protected during takeoff or landing.

features.name: Blueprints & Crafting
features.desc: For the first time, unique blueprints appear as rewards — specifically on higher difficulty tiers of Defend Location. They feed the crafting system introduced in 4.7.0.

features.name: Stability & Bug Fixes
features.desc: As a point release, 4.8.1 focused on stability improvements after 4.8.0 — fixes address ship claim issues, landing gear/hangar doors, and 60k shard lock issues.

4-8-2.tagline: Two flight-ready Gatac ships with grav-lev propulsion and the Alien Week celebration around First Contact Day.
4-8-2.summary: Point release with two new Gatac alien ships (Railen grav-lev freighter, Tyilui snub carrier) and the simultaneous launch of Alien Week 2956. The event celebrates xenotech manufacturers such as Gatac, Aopoa, Banu, and Esperia with time-limited store promotions and discounts. LTP active with a 20,000 aUEC starting balance; focus on stability and minor fixes to existing 4.8 systems.

keyFacts.label: Gatac Railen
keyFacts.value: ~640 SCU · grav-lev freighter

keyFacts.label: Gatac Tyilui
keyFacts.value: Snub carrier for quantum-incapable snub fighters

keyFacts.label: Alien Week 2956
keyFacts.value: June 17–25, 2026 · discounts on alien ships & equipment

keyFacts.label: First Contact Day
keyFacts.value: Annual day of remembrance for humanity's first contact with the Xi'an Empire

keyFacts.label: LTP Active
keyFacts.value: +20,000 aUEC starting balance

features.name: Gatac Railen
features.desc: Medium hybrid grav-lev freighter with around 640 SCU of cargo capacity, distributed across characteristic triangular cargo pods. It uses Xi'an graviton levitation technology instead of classic thrusters, which defines the alien-typical design and flight behavior.

features.name: Gatac Tyilui
features.desc: Snub carrier for quantum-incapable snub fighters. Inspired by science-fiction aesthetics (2001 style). It carries forward the mysterious, 2001: A Space Odyssey-inspired design philosophy of the Gatac line.

features.name: Alien Week 2956
features.desc: Annual event around First Contact Day (June 17–25, 2026) that puts xenotech manufacturers in the spotlight. Gatac is the 2956 headliner with the Railen, Syulen, and Tyilui. Accompanied by Pledge Store promotions, limited liveries, and alien lore features as well as Xi'an language lessons.

features.name: Grav-Lev Propulsion Technology
features.desc: Xi'an graviton levitation technology used in the Gatac ships. It replaces classic human thrusters and shapes the flight behavior and design aesthetics of the alien ships.

events.name: Alien Week 2956
events.desc: Celebration of the non-human manufacturers around First Contact Day. The Gatac ships Railen, Syulen, and Tyilui are the headliners. The event brings Pledge Store discounts on Gatac, Aopoa, Banu, and Esperia as well as limited liveries and alien gear.

events.name: First Contact Day
events.desc: Annual day of remembrance for the first contact between humanity and the Xi'an Empire. Alien Week honors this heritage with discounts on alien ships and equipment.

4-8-2.fixesNote: Further stability and bug fixes to 4.8.0 systems (exact number not specified).
4-8-2.wipe: LTP active · +20,000 aUEC starting balance (no full wipe)

4-8-3.tagline: The human-supremacist XenoThreat faction returns to Stanton as a dynamic combat event — July 1–15, 2026.
4-8-3.summary: Point release in the 4.8 line built entirely around the return of the XenoThreat event. The anti-alien, anti-UEE outlaws attack Stanton because the system supports coexistence with alien species — Foxwell Enforcement calls on players for help. The event runs as a points-based PvE combat event from July 1 to 15, 2026: across five mission types, players farm up to 120,000 event points and unlock blueprint rewards along the way. The centerpiece is the faction's Idris frigate as a capital-ship objective. Accompanied by stability and bug fixes to the existing 4.8 systems.

keyFacts.label: XenoThreat Returns
keyFacts.value: Dynamic PvE combat event in the Stanton system

keyFacts.label: Reward Track
keyFacts.value: 120,000 event points for the full reward track incl. blueprints

keyFacts.label: Faction
keyFacts.value: Human-supremacist, anti-alien outlaws (anti-UEE)

keyFacts.label: Contract Giver
keyFacts.value: Foxwell Enforcement

features.name: XenoThreat — Assault on Stanton
features.desc: The dynamic XenoThreat event returns to Stanton from July 1 to 15, 2026. The xenophobic outlaw faction attacks the system because it promotes coexistence with alien species. As a points-based combat event (not the old narrative event), players accept contracts, fight XenoThreat fleets, and work up the reward track toward 120,000 event points.

features.name: Five Mission Types
features.desc: The event offers five combat activities that award event points: defend alien-made ships under attack; eliminate anti-alien fighters; intercept and destroy exclusionary forces on arrival; draw out and defeat xenophobic lieutenants; and destroy or disable the Idris frigate.

features.name: Rewards & Blueprints
features.desc: Instead of the old narrative flow, 4.8.3 is a combat point farm: progress is tracked in the journal, and pushing all the way to the full 120,000-point reward track unlocks exclusive rewards including blueprints.

features.name: Idris Frigate
features.desc: The event's capital-ship objective: the XenoThreat fleet's Idris frigate can be neutralized either by destroying the ship or by killing its pilot — the toughest and most point-rich contract on the reward track.

facts.label: Completion
facts.value: Destroy the ship or eliminate the pilot

events.name: XenoThreat — Assault on Stanton
events.desc: Return of the dynamic XenoThreat event as a points-based combat event. The human-supremacist, anti-alien faction attacks Stanton over its support for alien coexistence; Foxwell Enforcement calls players in to help. Across five mission types — up to the Idris frigate — players farm up to 120,000 event points for blueprint rewards.

4-8-3.fixesNote: Accompanying stability and bug fixes to the 4.8 systems (number not specified).
4-8-3.wipe: No new wipe announced — continuation of the ongoing 4.8 cycle.
